text: Open Mosque invitations sent across multiple circles. Two RSVPs. The strategy isn't flawed—the relational density in those circles may not support conversion at this distance.
Hazim Maki calls about Malaysian Iftar. Tension surfaces: was supposed to reach out first, didn't. Being a hub means every dropped thread is visible.
Sister Afnan wanted a meeting. No clear time set. Relationship maintenance requires closure on every open loop. Ambiguity is relational debt.
David Hunt declines Open Mosque but requests next invite. The relationship survives the no. That's a bridge maintained, not a conversion lost.
MAPS Ready recruitment flows through Masoud and Hatim as nodes. They recruit Haythem, Ali Macky, Shaharyar. Network expansion depends on activating connectors, not being everywhere.
Imam Adam's circle outreach for Open Mosque—did he complete it. Delegation without confirmation creates phantom coverage. The map shows reach that may not exist.
Medical professionals circle identified. Mocktail event proposed to activate it. Notice how every relational opportunity gets immediately converted to event architecture.
Sister Mallak recruited for Open Mosque volunteers. No response on follow-up. When key nodes don't respond, is the ask too heavy or the relationship too thin.
Usrah committee attendance confirmations needed. Big event, need certainty. But uncertainty may be the signal—if commitment is soft, what does that reveal about the tie strength.
Indonesian and Malaysian circles documented separately. Both culturally aligned. Where's the bridge builder who can unlock cross-pollination without being the hub.
